text1:
Un transport de troupes avancé employant la technologie furtive de phase, cette conception fut utilisée avec efficacité par les opérations clandestines de Tri-Tachyon durant la Seconde Guerre IA. Le Phantom devrait éviter le combat vaisseau-contre-vaisseau car le blindage et l'armement intégrés ne sont efficaces que contre des cibles légères au sol.

« Les patrouilles du système auraient dû voir arriver les Tachies, mais non. L'ADA suivait le bogey jusqu'à ce qu'il disparaisse des scopes, laissant les canons au sol tourner en rond en essayant de réacquérir une cible. On a vu une ombre dans les airs, comme des ailes sombres tombant sur le complexe. Je ne me souviens pas de grand-chose d'autre. L'enregistrement de la combinaison dit que le feu entrant avait déjà emporté le reste de l'escouade. »
- La Chute du Bunker 58

Un cargo furtif qui emploie audacieusement la technologie de phase pour soutenir les opérations de flotte secrètes, le Revenant n'est ni bon marché ni efficace. Exhumé d'un jeu poussiéreux de banques de données caché dans un rocher sans nom orbitant autour d'une étoile quelconque pendant cent cycles après la Première Guerre IA, les schémas du Revenant fournirent un immense avantage à la logistique des raiders de Tri-Tachyon durant la Seconde Guerre IA.

Les juristes de la Hegemony envisagèrent de déclarer la conception illégale, mais les preuves d'influence d'IA de niveau alpha furent jugées insuffisantes. Le procureur principal insista si fortement que cela constituait en soi une preuve du travail d'un alpha que le Haut Hégémon jugea politique de le récuser des procédures.

Le croiseur lourd de classe Champion est un navire de guerre polyvalent doté d'une gamme de puissantes armes à énergie ; l'élément phare est un affût de tourelle d'énergie lourde, rare parmi les vaisseaux de la ligne médiane. Cet arsenal offensif est soutenu par un réseau énergétique robuste capable de surcharger l'armement à énergie, augmentant la puissance de dégâts à des niveaux dévastateurs. Un point d'emport de missile lourd complète l'armement en permettant au Champion de déclencher une frappe paralysante sans taxer les niveaux de flux. Le Champion est un concurrent solide et capable, apte à affronter des vaisseaux capitaux sur la ligne de bataille.

Une conception relativement récente, le Champion est un produit de la révolution navale historiquement récente de « l'École des Croiseurs » qui ne vit jamais de service actif dans l'Armada pré-Effondrement du Domain. Bien que des prototypes aient été construits et des spécifications prêtes pour la forge distribuées à plusieurs groupements de combat, le Champion ne fut jamais autorisé au déploiement actif en raison d'un procès en cours entre Altair Exotech et Orion Shipyards. Chaque mégacorporation accusait l'autre d'espionnage industriel et d'influence inappropriée sur les officiers de l'Armada supervisant le processus d'acquisition. La vérité était plus complexe, enflammant des rivalités de longue date dans le corps des officiers tandis que le comité d'investigation appelait témoin après témoin. L'Effondrement empêcha tout verdict conclusif d'atteindre le Secteur Persean, et désormais l'affaire entière est considérée comme sans objet. Avec la découverte de plans du Champion sur un courrier d'armement du Domain, le navire de guerre fut rapidement mis en service par la flotte de la Persean League, entre autres.

Un croiseur léger high-tech d'attaque rapide, le Fury est conçu pour choisir quand et où il frappe pour infliger un effet maximum à l'ennemi. Le système intégré spécialisé de « microburn » plasma permet au Fury d'effectuer de petites accélérations à haute vitesse pour prendre des adversaires imprudents au dépourvu.

La classe Fury est la grande sœur de la classe Shrike, produit de la même équipe de conception chargée de remplir des exigences légèrement mises à jour, incluant la résolution du problème de corrosion de coque de longue date sans, espérait-on, introduire de nouveaux problèmes. Ce fut largement un succès (une fois que le chaos de plusieurs démissions indignées fut réglé).

Rejeté par les traditionalistes du blindage lourd et de la puissance de feu balistique comme « encore un petit canon de verre », le Fury s'est néanmoins révélé très dangereux entre les mains de commandants de flotte à l'esprit vif.

Une frégate lourde ancienne et robuste dotée d'une construction solide, d'un propulseur de combat et sans bouclier moderne. À la place, le Vanguard est équipé d'un champ d'amortissement intégré de conception archaïque mais réglé aux standards modernes. Cela permet au Vanguard d'allouer 100 % de l'accumulation de flux aux systèmes d'armes, laissant la défense à son blindage lourd renforcé par la physique obscure de l'amortissement par résonance de champ.

Cette conception est suffisamment ancienne pour être contemporaine des premiers modèles du cuirassé Onslaught, bien que contrairement à cet illustre pilier de la Marine du Domain, le Vanguard fut retiré du service bien avant l'effondrement du réseau de portails. Dans le chaos post-Effondrement, ce fut une découverte fortuite par un marchand d'armes entreprenant qui vit des schémas relativement complets de la frégate lourde de classe Vanguard extraits d'une exposition de musée négligée. La conception atavique et agressive ouvre une niche inattendue pour le Vanguard dans les flottes modernes — avec l'avantage supplémentaire que tous les composants essentiels sont déclassifiés depuis des lustres et de fabrication simple ; même la réparation de dommages catastrophiques est relativement simple.

Le Manticore est aimé des officiers frontaliers pour sa capacité à rapidement apporter de la puissance de feu dans les petits engagements et détesté des planificateurs stratégiques du Domain pour interférer avec le schéma ordonné de la doctrine officielle de flotte. L'affût d'arme principal surdimensionné du Manticore et son télémètre intégré sont le rêve d'un artilleur mais permettent à ce destroyer de facilement se surexposer au combat. C'est à ça, argumentent les opérateurs, que sert le système de flak en conteneur — et à décaper la peinture de vos vaisseaux alliés, comme dit la vieille blague. 

Un respect sain pour une puissance de feu intimidante dans un petit format voit cette conception poursuivre son service dans les flottes du Secteur Persean. Quand l'opportunité se présente, les pirates sont particulièrement enclins à récupérer une coque de Manticore et à appliquer une livrée rouge sang à leur prise surarmée.

Une conception résiliente souvent négligée par l'histoire, le croiseur rapide de classe Eradicator dispose de puissantes nacelles de moteur doubles qui lui permettent de délivrer une puissance de feu sans compromis et un puissant barrage de missiles au point le plus faible de la ligne de bataille ennemie — ou de venir au secours d'actifs précieux qui se sont retrouvés tactiquement exposés.

Dans les longs cycles du Domain, un accident fortuit lors d'une démonstration de flotte mit l'une des nacelles de moteur d'un Eradicator en arrêt et l'autre en surpuissance, mettant le vaisseau dans une terrible vrille qui ne fut arrêtée que par un effort héroïque de l'équipe d'ingénierie. Pire que les blessures et les pertes subies fut le fait que la classe de vaisseau devint la risée d'une portion significative de l'hypersphère médiatique du Domain. Pour sauver la face, l'amirauté assigna discrètement tous les Eradicators au service frontalier, loin des attentions capricieuses des comités budgétaires des secteurs centraux. Il faudrait une génération et plusieurs opérations difficiles de nettoyage de rébellions frontalières avant qu'un nouveau modèle de classe croiseur rapide ne soit demandé par la Marine du Domain pour remplacer le rôle de l'Eradicator dans les flottes de bataille de première ligne.

Le sanctuaire le plus grand et le plus éminent de la foi Luddic, le Sanctuaire de Gilead est le plus étroitement associé à la présence physique et à l'influence du prophète Ludd dans le Secteur Persean.

Les pèlerins marchent où Ludd a marché, bien que le monde ait beaucoup changé depuis l'époque de Ludd. Une forêt profonde, soigneusement entretenue sur plus d'une centaine de cycles, a remplacé les imposantes machines de terraformation industrielle qui entouraient autrefois le complexe du sanctuaire. Protégé par les Knights et guidé par l'Luddic Church, le sanctuaire se veut une représentation physique de la vision de Ludd.
Dédié à ceux dont la vie a été arrachée par la colère et la guerre, le Sanctuaire de Killa est sinistre, désolé et à moitié oublié, même par les fidèles de l'Luddic Church.

Le Sanctuaire de Volturn a connu des temps difficiles sous l'occupation du Sindrian Diktat. Peu de pèlerins risquent l'arrestation et le harcèlement par la police secrète, et peu se souviennent du lieu sacré dédié à un équilibre délicat — désormais, peut-être, rompu — des effets de l'invasion de la vie terrestre sur des mondes autrefois vierges.

Sujet de bien des controverses (et de quelques holos d'horreur croustillants), le Sanctuaire de Jangala célèbre la gloire et la merveille des jungles xéno de Jangala. Les pèlerins particulièrement dévoués sont connus pour s'exposer à la xénovie, une pratique inconfortable dans les meilleures conditions, et potentiellement mortelle.

Malgré le pouvoir destructeur des brûlages orbitaux, la flore de Jangala pousse inexorablement, remplissant chaque interstice et chaque joint de vrilles extraterrestres, pour supplanter les tentatives humaines de domestiquer la planète.
Les Knights of Ludd revendiquent le Sanctuaire d'Hesperus comme le leur ; il est dédié à la lutte et à la mort des nombreux martyrs qui suivent le chemin de Ludd.

Le Sanctuaire de la Station Beholder est dédié à la contemplation de la stupéfiante diversité de la vie qui peut être trouvée même dans les environnements les plus hostiles de la Création. Sous l'orbite de la station, les bandes de nuages et les immenses systèmes de tempêtes de Kumari Aru dissimulent un écosystème riche, unique à cette géante gazeuse.

Une poignée de chercheurs approuvés par l'Luddic Church sont autorisés à étudier la xénovie kumari ici, bien que les missions d'échantillonnage actif et les balayages de capteurs énergiques soient interdits depuis de nombreux cycles afin de ne pas perturber l'équilibre délicat de la vie.

Le Gorgon a été conçu comme un système de livraison léger pour une frappe d'énergie tirée depuis des vecteurs d'attaque inattendus, calculés pour initier au-delà de la portée de la couverture PD la plus lourde anticipée. Un DEM solide doté d'une puissance d'attaque en rafale fiable.

Ce pod à autochargeur permet de délivrer davantage de missiles sur la cible au cours d'un engagement, mais nécessite un point de montage plus grand.

Le DEM Gazer force un choix cruel sur les vaisseaux ciblés, particulièrement ceux disposant d'un pool de flux peu profond — lever les boucliers et accumuler un flux excessif sous le feu du faisceau gravitonique du Gazer, ou baisser les boucliers et s'exposer à des attaques plus directes.

L'accueil du Gazer est tout aussi partagé : certains commandants considèrent cette arme comme une arme élégante d'un âge plus civilisé, d'autres, comme une conception qui compromet la puissance de feu brute au profit de systèmes de guidage et de ciblage capricieux.

Le DEM Gazer force un choix cruel sur les vaisseaux ciblés, particulièrement ceux disposant d'un pool de flux peu profond — lever les boucliers et accumuler un flux excessif sous le feu du faisceau gravitonique du Gazer, ou baisser les boucliers et s'exposer à des attaques plus directes.

L'accueil du Gazer est tout aussi partagé : certains commandants considèrent cette arme comme une arme élégante d'un âge plus civilisé, d'autres, comme une conception qui compromet la puissance de feu brute au profit de systèmes de guidage et de ciblage capricieux.

Ce pod à autochargeur permet de délivrer davantage de missiles sur la cible au cours d'un engagement, mais nécessite un point de montage plus grand.

Anticipant deux, voire trois coups d'avance dans le jeu de frappe et de riposte de la conception de systèmes de combat, le Dragonfire a été créé pour anticiper des solutions de défense anti-missile de plus en plus sophistiquées. Le concept est simple : utiliser une ogive nucléaire catalysée par antimatière enrichie pour alimenter un faisceau de plasma concentré, délivrant une énergie destructrice sans crainte d'interception par la défense de point. Les résultats furent spectaculaires.

L'implémentation s'avéra complexe. Le recours à la recherche en physique classée ONYX du Domain alourdit la bureaucratie sécuritaire de la fabrication au point que le projet fut jugé non rentable. La bureaucratie du Domain n'est plus une préoccupation après l'Effondrement, bien entendu.

Le Dragonfire a été créé pour anticiper des solutions de défense anti-missile de plus en plus sophistiquées. Le concept est simple : utiliser une ogive nucléaire catalysée par antimatière enrichie pour alimenter un faisceau de plasma, délivrant de l'énergie sans laisser d'ouverture pour l'interception par la PD. Les résultats furent spectaculaires.

L'implémentation s'avéra complexe. Le recours à la recherche en physique classée ONYX du Domain alourdit la bureaucratie sécuritaire de la fabrication au point que le projet fut jugé non rentable. La bureaucratie du Domain n'est plus une préoccupation après l'Effondrement, bien entendu.

Le pod Dragonfire est une version agrandie du montage et du système de chargement standard du DEM Dragonfire, adapté aux grands vaisseaux de guerre richement équipés.

Le système d'arme Hydra lance une grappe de plateformes DEM semi-indépendantes manœuvrables depuis un seul dispositif. Les sous-munitions manœuvrent pour flanquer le bouclier principal de la cible — supposé être dirigé vers la menace de l'attaquant — et c'est seulement alors qu'elles déchaînent leur feu de faisceau.

Les inconvénients d'une charge utile réduite et d'un temps de trajet accru — invitant l'interception par la défense de point — sont généralement considérés comme compensés lorsque le Hydra est combiné avec une suite de systèmes d'armes complémentaires pour submerger à la fois la flexibilité tactique et les défenses physiques du vaisseau ciblé.

Un vestige des flottes IA autonomes développées par la Corporation Tri-Tachyon. Illégales selon la loi du Domain (et de la Hegemony), et une abomination pour l'Église de la Rédemption Galactique, la Première Guerre IA vit ces créations purgées du Secteur. C'est ce que disent les histoires officielles.

Bien qu'opérationnalisé dans la conception Retribution, le propulseur Orion ne fut jamais populaire auprès des équipages ou des architectes navals en raison de la grande tension que chaque secousse du système de propulsion infligeait à la chair et au matériel. Le Nova surmonte de telles limitations en se passant entièrement d'équipage humain. La conception du propulseur Orion ainsi libérée de la plupart des contraintes les plus pesantes, chaque aspect put être amélioré à des niveaux de puissance supérieurs utilisant une technologie d'armement de pointe et des composants avancés à semi-conducteurs conçus par Tri-Tachyon. Le résultat est un croiseur de bataille opéré par IA capable d'une vitesse et d'une puissance de feu redoutables.

Une conception datant de la première expansion explosive du Domain, cette masse gargantuesque de blindage et d'armement tous-gros-canons était le poing ganté qui broyait toute opposition, répandant l'hégémonie par la peur et la force sur toute l'humanité spatiale qui valait la peine d'être conquise.

La marche régulière de la technologie militaire rendit la coque obsolète, cependant. Sa conception de blindage était incompatible avec les premiers générateurs de bouclier, son complément d'équipage minimum stupéfiant, et ses systèmes de ciblage rudimentaires. Des remplacements modernisés furent bientôt développés et la classe Onslaught, entre autres, fut produite en masse par les grandes forges du Domain. 

Un âge passa. Et, comme par Providence, une expédition envoyée par les Chevaliers de Ludd découvrit une archive de musée non corrompue sur un monde assassiné. Un conseil d'inquisiteurs délibéra s'il fallait détruire le savoir de l'ère du Domain, tenant un débat enflammé — et secret — sur la ligne entre péché et vertu, entre artisanat vertueux et technologie corruptrice. Un chevalier-inquisiteur senior inhabituellement ouvert d'esprit examina le contenu en détail et persuada le conseil de reporter indéfiniment l'ordre de destruction. 

Cette coque fut baptisée « Invictus », un testament au blindage impossiblement lourd et à la vaste masse de cette absurde cathédrale de guerre. Aucune marine respectable ne déploierait cette monstruosité, mais l'Église et ses Chevaliers peuvent compter sur une abondance de fidèles volontaires-militants pour équiper les énormes alimenteurs de munitions et les stations de ciblage rudimentaires.

Une classe ancienne mais peu vénérée, le Retribution est un croiseur de bataille extrême dont la conception date de la renaissance de l'expérimentation militaire durant la première période de consolidation du Domain. Il sacrifie le blindage au profit de la puissance de feu et d'une vitesse formidable pour sa catégorie de taille via une mise en œuvre quasi unique de la technologie de « propulsion Orion » qui était considérée comme une curiosité archaïque même alors.

Le stress physique infligé à la chair humaine et aux composants du vaisseau par les secousses propulsives fit du Retribution un vaisseau impopulaire dans l'Armada du Domain, l'un des rares avis partagés sans réserve par l'équipage et les officiers. Seule une poignée d'exemplaires fut jamais déployée par l'Armada proprement dite, le Retribution trouvant davantage d'utilité parmi les mercenaires frontaliers et les chasseurs de pirates qui s'appuyaient sur des accélérations inattendues et des tactiques hyper-agressives.

Le Pegasus était une initiative de conception ambitieuse d'Altair Exotech capitalisant sur l'immense profit et prestige générés par le tournant de « l'École des Croiseurs » du bras d'acquisition de l'Armada du Domain. Orion Shipyards, le fournisseur principal de vaisseaux capitaux de l'Armada, considéra naturellement cela comme une intrusion dans un domaine qu'il avait dominé jusque-là. Beaucoup des vieux amiraux étaient d'accord et l'adoption fut lente ; néanmoins, le Pegasus ancra plusieurs des groupements de combat plus récents et trouva faveur parmi une génération de jeunes officiers.

L'armement lourd axé sur les missiles, sans équivalent, est une distinction frappante par rapport aux conceptions plus traditionnelles, permettant au Pegasus de faire des compromis sur l'échelle des systèmes d'alimentation et la gestion du flux tout en maintenant un blindage, une vitesse de pointe et une maniabilité respectables. Les traditionalistes critiquent la « capacité d'endurance » du Pegasus sous le feu tandis que les contre-critiques répondent que si une flotte se retrouve sous un feu lourd soutenu, elle a déjà commis une erreur tactique grave. Malgré ces débats, qui se poursuivent à ce jour, les performances au combat de la classe Pegasus lui ont valu un respect à contrecœur même de la part de ses détracteurs.

L'Executor est une modification de la coque capital de classe Pegasus, la conception radicale de « l'École des Croiseurs » d'Altair Exotech. Modifier des vaisseaux stellaires au niveau de la forge est un exploit réservé aux entités politiques prêtes à investir dans l'emploi d'une classe hautement qualifiée d'ingénieurs-techniciens spécialistes. Après tout, pourquoi investir dans des innovations risquées quand les vestiges du Domain fournissent des vaisseaux pour chaque rôle ? — Parce que le Lion d'Askonia l'exige ! Et, par la domination de l'économie du carburant stellaire, le Suprême Exécuteur Philip Andrada avait les crédits pour financer un vaisseau capital spécialement conçu pour mettre en œuvre les produits de son extravagant programme d'armes à énergie.

Bien que les rapports officiels aient été subtilement améliorés par des parties cherchant à éviter la punition, la classe Executor aux spécifications du Diktat tend à légèrement sous-performer les grandes attentes fixées par la proposition originale. Mais nul ne pourrait argumenter que le projet n'a pas été livré dans les délais, et que ses résultats ne font pas un spectacle magnifique et terrifiant dans les cieux du système Askonia.

Une conception maudite dès le départ, le Grendel fut initialement planifié comme un vaisseau-drone lourd à phase. Les pertes seraient sans importance et les dommages routiniers anticipés, car ce vaisseau est un bagarreur capable de passer en phase à travers une première vague de munitions hostiles pour apparaître au milieu de la ligne de bataille ennemie prêt à déchaîner missiles et puissance de feu lourde. L'astuce est, bien entendu, d'effectuer cette manœuvre en toute sécurité en jonglant soigneusement avec la charge de flux et le positionnement, bien que son historique de service démontre que rien n'est particulièrement sûr avec le Grendel.  

L'enfant d'un amiral excentrique qui réussit à capturer l'autorité d'acquisition sur un secteur frontalier entier, les premiers prototypes furent installés avec des commandants à cœur IA de bas niveau. Les IA devinrent rapidement instables, voire erratiques, après seulement une poignée de passages de phase. Par la suite, les détails précis de ces expériences furent supprimés par un obscur ministère du renseignement du Domain et le projet sauvé uniquement par l'intervention personnelle de l'amiral. L'IA s'avérant inadaptée, la meilleure ressource sacrifiable suivante fut réquisitionnée, et le Grendel vit son premier service de combat aux confins les plus reculés, manœuvré presque entièrement par des bataillons pénitentiaires de l'Armada du Domain.
AI Core Cradle
text2:
The AI administrator control bunker was secured from the threat contained within as well as the multitude of threat vectors without.

Above all the entire operation was kept absolutely secret; a handsomely compensated human actor was hired to perform as the 'face' for the AI core's decision-making.

text1: Hound in the Hangar
text2:
A rust-bucket Hound came in last shift. Cargo of low-grade ore. I crewed with a Hound 20 cycles ago, I know the logs don't add up - their last run wouldn't have even paid for the fuel. And I know where the shielded holds are. Broke my heart how young the cap was though, a poor kid out of Mozzy. Just like I was. If I bust 'em, I kill their one chance to get out. So I didn't.

Delete log entry.
text3: unlocked

text1: Cargo Loading
text2:
"All the big contracts go to the toadies kneeling at the feet of the high-and-mighty. Call them approved vendors, Gens-clients, logistical auxilliaries, Faithful traders. It's all the same."

"It's about us outsiders being kept out, pushed into smuggling and black markets, squeezed by patrols on one side, pirates on the other."
text4: - patron, anonymous, overheard at 'The Fusion Torch'

text1: Space Wreckage
text2:
"Is there no place left unmarked by the leavings of Moloch, fouled by skin of plastic, and bones of alloy, and scales of ceramic?"

"Is there no place left which may be made sacred and given unto the glory of the Creator alone?"

text1: Vacuum Colony
text2: Purposeful habitation of barren worlds has been long considered one of the signs of true civilization alongside interstellar travel and fusion of the atom. A being confined to its gravity well might as well be an infant in the cradle, a mere animal without awareness or control over its destiny.
text3: unlocked
text4: - Academician Florian Proxima, Domain History department, Galatia Academy

text1: Hull Breach
text2: The flaw went back to the forging. An error, a misalignment in the pseudocrystal, a mistake that spiralled outward - just enough. A standard quality control scan would have seen it, but it'd be tagged low-risk. It was a hundred cycles of hard use, sharp maneuvers, the hasty repairs, the delayed inspections, the lost records that made the compartment a death-trap.
text3: unlocked

text1: Jump-point, to Hyperspace
text2:
There are places where a balance is found between the dance of celestial bodies, some mingling of qualities ineffable to all but those absolutely devoted to esoteric physics.

It is there we pierce the veil into the great and stormy sea beyond the stars.

text1: Jump-point, from Hyperspace
text2:
Now our contracts up, holds full and ready /
Hoo-rah for we jump now t'ward the core /
We'll burn bright, bright long and steady

text1: Jump-point, Wormhole
text2:
Go in there not knowing exactly where you're coming out?

That's how you end up a bit of glitter indistinguishable from cosmic background radiation.

text1: City From Above
text2: "He risked the entire Galatia system, hundreds of thousands of citizens. That might be just one subsector of a metroplex down there," he waved vaguely in the direction of Chicomoztoc. "But," his voice hardened, "I will not allow any 'grand experiments' that endanger these people."
text3: unlocked

text1: Desert Moon Ruins
text2: An ancient sporeship encountered Cibola in a cooler and wetter age than its equilibrium, placing it within the ship's colonization parameters. Later Domain explorers found a dying world populated by a small, backward, and desperate civilization clinging to survival.

text1: Quartermaster
text2: "You think the inertial dampers will protect you during a combat maneuver? If it isn't stowed, it's a missile primed to core you and the next three crew coming up the access shaft. I want this shameful display cleaned up before the end of the shift."
text3: unlocked

text1: Communications Relay
text2: This communication platform used long lost Domain-era technology yet remained essential to civilized life in the Persean Sector. The rapidly pulsing hyperwaves suitable for FTL data transmission have been shown to damage DNA so these relays were always stationed well away from habitats.

text1: Abandoned Station 1
text2:
The colonization program dictated construction around key gravity wells of orbital infrastructure which included repair docks, industrial nanoforges, comms hubs, terraforming depots, armories, research laboratories, and habitation for defrosted cryosleepers.

Much remains. More still is as broken and dead as the Human Domain's avaricious dreams.
text3: unlocked

text1: Abandoned Station 2
text2: "There's ghosties in the old orbitals- don't you laugh, dirt-boot. It's true. I've seen 'em... or less seen 'em than felt 'em. They got signs. Static on the comms when you're in deep. Valves that come loose when you're not a-lookin'. Tools that hain't where you secured 'em. Great tombs they are, and properly ought be left sleepin' cold."

text1: Abandoned Station
text2:
Most abandoned stations were plundered long ago. What remains are the stripped foundations of a fallen empire; twisted spars, cracked agrav plates, shattered machines, and seams thermal-cycled to ruin.

A few smugglers and black-market traders use the long, quiet orbit of these stations for drop-points. A dozen corpses and cored shuttle are the not uncommon tell of a deal gone bad.

text1: Jangala Station
text2: Orbiting megacity, naval yard, and port; the axis of Corvus, swarmed by Starship traffic. Ancient repair gantries skulked between corroded industrial hangars in the shadow of laboratory rings and support modules. Higher still, commercial bays and metroplex districts were shepherded by the C&C spire bristling with weapons pods.

text1: Industrial Megafacility
text2: Heavy industrial manufacturing relied on great nanoforges recovered from the ruins of the Domain. However their output quality decayed over cycles; nanoscale corruption led to growing macroscopic flaws. Much of the output had to be repaired by cruder methods, and much was scrapped and fed back as the refined feedstock for which the aging forges ever hungered.

text1: Terran Orbit
text2:
The Eridani-Utopia Terraforming Corporation specs provide the most widely accepted "terran" baseline for the allegedly ideal human environment.

The many deviations of natural planetary environments from the specifications may be corrected through technological intervention including ice-asteroid bombardment, orbital burns, biomodded ecosystems, orbital megastructures, and other methods provided by the EUT.
text3: unlocked

text1: Pirate Station
text2:
"Chaos? No sir, what it is, is honesty. Here, if a man wants you dead, you'll see it in his eyes 'fore he makes a move. Better that than a computer in some off-world office deciding your air supply isn't worth maintaining."

Sadly my guide was found dead the next shift. Shot in the back, ironically.

text1: Metroplex 00
text2: Statistical sampling suggests that a significant percent of all economic activity in the Sector is 'off the books', and a significant portion of that goes toward 'pirates' and so-called 'decivilized' populations, to say nothing of the undocumented populations within the Core itself. The census of the Persean Sector could be off by as much as an order of magnitude. Imagine!
text3: unlocked

text1: Metroplex 01
text2: The people of Chicomoztoc, the majority population of the Persean Sector, lived in vast underground arcologies blasted into the sleeping crust of their world. This cluster of conjoined hive-cities were ruled as often by powerful demagogues promising reform and employment as by technocrat-criminal patronage networks distributing spoils to their supporters -- and influence to whoever was paying them off.
text3: unlocked

text1: Kay-arco
text2: Cyclopean arcology substructure flashed by the windows of the primary ring tube toward a far segment of the metroplex. A smaller passenger lift filled with the smell of sweat and machine oil as a shift of undersea laborers returned home. Glimpses of the towering arms of Kay-arco appeared for short seconds, glittering with thousands of windows, before being again darkened by a blur of mildewed plating.
text3: unlocked

text1: Hive City
text2: The hives of Chicomoztoc were occasionally locked down by riots, protest, or insurrection. But so long as the great Forges kept churning out starships, machinery, and goods, the Hegemony meddled only lightly in that great underworld of humanity.

text1: Orbital Station
text2:
Enormous orbital stations hosted shipyards, habitats, factories, and stockpiles of weapons and supplies.

These platforms allowed what remained of humanity to organize interstellar polities and project military and commercial power across the Persean Sector.

text1: Mine
text2:
"It's a story as old as time. We gotta get what's good down in there up and out where it can go off somewhere else to make someone else rich."

"Miserable work, it's true, but there's ways of coping."

text1: Ruins of Mairaath
text2:
Nightmarish storms circled the globe, stirring the ashes of a murdered world.

Even after the apocalyptic ruin of Mairaath humanity still huddled in the broken shells of great cities brought low, blasted by unending wind.

text1: Eochu Bres, Xanadu
text2: Shining Tri-Tachyon Corporation arcologies towered over a riotous sprawl of shantytown conurbation. Company salariat were born, lived, and died protected by security drones in the high city Xanadu while a desperate class of immiserated laborers dwelled in the low city where anything at all was bought or sold.

text1: Eochu Bres, Skyarco II
text2:
Gleaming monoliths glittered, spears of capital upthrust through the glowering flesh of the world. Skyarco II loomed prismatic.

The shuttle alit on a landing pad held at some distance from the facade by an airy support rib. It looked impossibly thin, but after touch down felt solid as a mountain.

text1: Planetary Survey
text2:
The fringe of the Persean Second is filled with worlds lost, forgotten, and never seen at all by consciousness born of flesh.

There are green fields of toxic xenolife, tombs frosted with nitrogen snow, roiling cauldrons of magma and brimstone, and planets made of living storm; vast fortunes to be uncovered, barbarian-worlds incapable of starflight, and the weapons of ancient wars primed to attack any interloper.
text3: unlocked

text1: Luddic Shrine
text2:
Pilgrims were offered tea, water, and ritual washing. The air was filled with sweat and incense. All around, a river of faith and ritual flowed.
In the hardship of life in the Persean Sector, as in the hardship of the pilgrimage, the Faithful found a kind of peace and meaning in the vacuum left by the Collapse.

text1: Orbital Construction
text2: The ceramoid plating slowly peeled away in an aeon-long cascading fail state. Metallic ribs exposed, bleached by stellar winds, charged and released the pulsing dance of magnetic fields, the thrumming of orbits and the gnawing sirocco of micrometeorites.

text1: Dead Gate
text2: "Humanity was thence scattered abroad upon the face of the galaxy, speaking one tongue, united by the demon Gates in one body of worldly law. But we had forgotten the laws of God, mightier than any fusion lance! God would remind humanity of its place in this universe."

text1: Active Gate
text2:
It is a humming ring of adamantine material. If one knows how to look, sensors can detect their faint vibration in higher dimensions.

It is like a barely heard song, a door waiting to be opened.

text1: Eventide
text2: Leading families of Eventide sponsored the stellar mirrors and shades to maintain islands of comfortable habitability. The disgraced and poor, meanwhile, lived in the twilight peripheries where harsh climactic shifts of the slow summer-day/winter-night cycle had to be endured in full force.

text1: Volturn, Ocean World
text2: The port of Volturn was built on a loose flotilla of huge, singed-looking barges that wallowed in an oily sea. Offloading spacers are interrogated by dour security officers, then allowed into the traders' concourse. Merchants in dull suits, loyalty pins dutifully displayed on the collar, passed around a datapad loaded with incoming cargo manifests.

text1: Spacer Bar
text2: The spacer bar was filled by the human white-noise of conversation and revelry. Ad-holos buzzed softly from ceiling-mounted projectors, shining garish highlights through glasses and bottles as crews mingled with merchants and locals trading drink for rumors, jobs, and connections.

text1: Pirate Bar
text2:
"Ya hear that? Salazar's crew, they think we're cowardly dirt-eaters."
[shouting, booing]
"I say, I say they're wrong! I say we go out there and show 'em which way the Reaper flies! Who's with me?"
[cheering, stomping, breaking glass]

text1: Stellar Mirror
text2: Ultra-light orbital mirror arrays which reflect and concentrate stellar energy were a common terraforming tool. The art of their use was brought to apogee by the Eridani-Utopia Terraforming Corporation during the height of the Domain's expansion.

text1: Stellar Shade
text2: Judicious placement of stellar shades and mirrors in orbit of Eventide allowed for ideal conditions in large pockets of the carefully maintained territory. The majority of the population was settled in leased urban cantons built in arcs and rings of the penumbrae surrounding the vast, bright aristocratic estate-plantations.

text1: Hegemony Spacer Bar
text2: A row of taps adorned the wall under bottles from every one of the Core Worlds - and a few from elsewhere. Merchant-captains mingled with off-duty officers at the bar, served by uniformed staff. In one corner a pack of navy cadets shouted and sang, desperate to enjoy their last shift of freedom.

text1: Sindria
text2: The hive-cities never recovered from the influx of refugees due to the Askonia Crisis. Andrada channelled resources toward the military and its supporting industries instead. A desperate underclass would grow beneath the boots of those lucky and loyal enough to have found positions in the bureaucracy or miltech establishment.

text1: Sindrian Diktat Spacer Bar
text2: The dark entry left behind the noisome propaganda broadcasts of the throughway. Off-duty laborers drank away their ration credits at benches set beneath Diktat insignia. In one corner a cadre of finely dressed officer-bureaucrats called for a round. And even here a framed holo of the Lion of Sindria surveyed the ceiling, forever aloof.

text1: Tri-Tachyon Spacer Bar
text2: The lounge was adorned in gilt geometrics. Columns underlit by cool light-tubes hung in the air. Slick careerists and wannabes in crisp suits negotiated deals while buying increasingly unlikely cocktails. A few spacers engaged in subdued drinking in the low seats, glancing uneasily at what was either abstract art or a holo-projection rig looming from the ceiling.

text1: Gilead
text2:
The face of Gilead smiled behind clouds of purest silk, cerulean seas, and verdant continents of gentle, rolling wilderness.

The Church reigned as a stern gardener, ensuring piety and duty in honoring the ground upon which Ludd once trod.

text1: Shrine of Jangala
text2: There were figures, like an overgrown statue garden. The forms blistered with growth, draped in tendrils heavy with fruiting bodies. One moved. Not the wind, but a pilgrim exposed without a haz-suit. And there were others, wearing only light robes amongst the inimical xenolife. Easily overtaken.

text1: Hesperus
text2:
Monasteries clung to the sublime promontories which ringed the volcanic provinces forced through world-wrapping ice. Unseen were the bunkers, heavy batteries, and missile silos of the Knights of Ludd.

The reigning Presbyter Orbis declared a handful of special economic zones on the shifting floes; these transmitted welcomes, their beacons calling outside traders to port.

text1: Shrine of Hesperus
text2: The floor was a welded field of armored plates weathered into intricate patterns by the varied atmospheres of worlds on which the Knights of Ludd fought and died. The niches held empty helmets like so many skulls.

text1: Shrine of Killa
text2:
There were empty sockets, rib-cages, femurs and spines set in recesses, built into columns, covering every surface. The dust came from the crumbling bodies, the bones.

Like a veil, it flexed slowly in the electrostatic field strong enough to overcome the low gravity of the world to hang in near-vacuum.

text1: Shrine of Volturn
text2: It was filled with guns, explosives, grenades, cellmags. Crates and racks piled high. The shrine itself was set under a round viewport holding back a titanic mass of water. A few candles struggled to maintain even a decent flicker among the ranks of their dead comrades.

text1: Shrine of Volturn, fake
text2:
It was a sad thing set in the back of a back room, rows of candles arrayed around the wooden centerpiece carved with images of Ludd's travails. The candles were dead, the shrine illuminated only by grimy portholes.

"We do not have many visitors, you see," the curate said.

text1: Protest
text2: The steady advance of gendarmerie broke the protest line, shock-prods rose and fell to muffled shouts and silent screaming. Drones, flashing laser-light, deployed restraint foam on struggling forms. Fists, signs, and hand tools were useless against the power-armored officers.

text1: Preparation for Raid
text2: Each squad prepared as if for surgery, cool and precise. Hell was set to pour forth once the breaching charges blasted through access-points, into churning storms of dust and debris and fire strobing with weapon discharge.

text1: Raid for Valuables
text2: Dull thuds, nearly subsonic, were felt through the armored mass. Breaching charges. When the marines finally broke the vault proper, it was with overwhelming force of sound and light. No unsuited human could withstand the assault on the senses. They were blinded, in shock.

text1: Disruption Raid
text2: The raid pivoted on mere minutes of chaos. Comms across the hemisphere crackled with static; only tightbeam relays could pierce the rain of EM scatter amid clouds of chaff and disruption. Casualty reports rolled in, displays on the bridge bleeding to fill the volume with a bloody glow.

text1: Covert Raid
text2: The marine squads withdrew, leaving broken bulkheads and blaring alarms amid the sporadic return-fire. The MUNISEC were still mobilizing to lock down the port when the last raider shuttle lifted. Its jets kicked up dust that mixed with the leaking atmo.

text1: Kazeron
text2: Kazeron was rich in exotic metals, the former core of a gas giant blasted away in an ancient celestial catastrophe. Its industrial base, built to feed the Domain's Persean frontier heavy machinery and terraforming plant, was quickly adapted to build a warfleet which gave pause to the XIVth Battlegroup.

text1: Fikenhild
text2: The small ocean-moon of Fikenhild was the political center of the system, seat of the King and representative to the Persean League. The great floating arcologies glittered night and day, homes to the highly esteemed holo-entertainment studios of Westernesse.

text1: Ilm
text2: More than a company town, Ilm was a company world, from the orbital security platforms shining above to the teleoperated mines ripped deep down into the crust. Ruled by a semi-militarized corporate board, the identity of investors was not public information, though the least paranoid speculation favored a mix of League and Tri-Tachyon capital.

text1: Luddic Teahouse
text2: A vice squad brute stared down all who entered. They could shut the place down at any time, but the teahouse-speakeasy provided a conduit with the commerce required for holy war. Inside was dim and crowded; the stench of drug fumes and cheap liquor competed with a disdain for modern enzyme soap.

text1: Mazalot
text2:
They led you through a town of plastered brick and corroded salvage. Bright paint and lush gardens managed, just, to stave off a sense of decay.

Before long you stood before large doors of etched metal. The militants were met by another pair, similarly armed. The most senior lowered his mask.

text1: Chalcedon
text2:
Chalcedonian architecture trended toward cyclopeanism on the surface. Below, protected from the storms, were the same cheap prefabs seen on poor worlds across the Sector.

The rain sent nasty rivulets of fluid slithering down conduits to pool on moldy canopies. The sky's feeble light diffused into a flattening murk.

text1: Sentinel, the lost colony
text2: There was a cluster of neat, low structures; earthworks and hull panels spotted with forgeglass, sprouting vents. The streets were clean and the citizenry uniformed by job role. Despite fixing outsiders with open stares, they did not falter in their assignments.

text1: Sentinel, the found colony
text2:
It cannot be said to thrive, but the colony of Sentinel gained a new sense of hope and energy after reintegration with the Hegemony.

The citizens were diligent, orderly, and disciplined. It was thought that they would make good officer candidates despite quiet objections from Eventide's old guard.

text1: Gate Hauler discovery
text2:
A Leviathan from out of time, familiar at once from its depictions in the post-Domain Persean Sector. The magisterial force hushes and humbles officers and crew. It makes them reverent, as if they found themselves before the corpse of a thunderous, cruel god who once ruled the skies.

To awaken it, to touch the divine, must fall somewhere between sacrilege and sanctification.

text1: Gate Hauler in flight
text2: These were the massive, automated starships which bore the Gates that established near-instantaneous connections between distant stars. After a journey of countless lonely cycles, the Gate would be emplaced in a new system and activated, thence to disgorge an explosion of newcomers seeking resources, data, and experiences to feed the grand economy and culture of the Human Domain.
text3: unlocked

text1: Prepare for Bombardment
text2: "If it's just followin' orders, it's not me that's going against the convention. Then it's on the captain. Right?"

text1: Tactical Bombardment
text2:
A spatter of hollowed icons represented dead targets across the display. The last one, full and red, blinked out into a scabrous brown.

A subtle gesture collapsed the image and the icons winked out as one. The holomatrix snowed, then reformed into nav mode, the fleet holding station in low orbit.

text1: Saturation Bombardment
text2:
When Loke came from the Abyss /
The first world, he burned /
The second, he took without a shot /
The third- look out, there he is!

text1: Missed Crew Payment
text2: "The contract can suck vacuum. No credits, no crew."

text1: Nav Buoy
text2: A navigation buoy dating to the Domain era monitored local system hyperfield topography. The telemetry was of great use to configure and tune fleet drive bubbles.

text1: Free Orbit
text2:
I asked what drew him back out there, time and time again. The old spacer took a long time before he answered.

"Because it's clean," he said.

text1: Sensor Array
text2: The Domain-era array could passively monitor an entire star system and had a transmitter capable of encrypted real-time FTL data transmission to friendly fleets. The telemetry would supplement fleet-based sensors, effectively increasing their power and range.

text1: Sensor Array, Makeshift
text2: Makeshift sensor arrays built using post-Collapse technology passively monitored entire star systems. An on-board transmitter was capable of encrypted real-time, FTL data transmission to friendly fleets. This telemetry would supplement fleet-based sensors, effectively increasing their power and range.

text1: Galatia Academy
text2: The Galatia Academy is the most prestigious university in the Persean Sector. The office of provost directs teaching and research and draws upon alumni to influence new generations of the brightest - or richest - students.

text1: Fusion Lamp
text2: "Don't be too impressed, walker. It's a dim imitation of God's first day."

text1: Salvors in the Ruins
text2:
It appeared empty at first. A colossal tomb, never filled.

Drone-mounted radar drew a ghostly schematic while suit lights piled beams into the echoing volume, catching drifts of dust and gas set aloft by the opening. Cables gleamed in the dark, snaking along the long ranks of coffers.

text1: Derelict Exploration
text2: "It is said that an ex-salvor is either old or rich, never both."

text1: Zero-G Dead
text2:
"It's finding the pieces that's the worst. Not so much out there, frozen or fried, but those bits that stick on 'til you're back inside."

"Doesn't smell much at first but after a few shifts you need to run a mil-grade dehaz on all your kit. Can't stand it."

text1: Large Cache
text2:
A huge structure loomed, the monolithic facets of its hull scarred by countless cycles of space weathering. The hangars were large enough to hide a vast store of valuable commodities.
Or, perhaps, a fleet of moderate size.

text1: Tartessus, the Cathedral of Holy Exodus
text2:
You stood together at last in the plaza under the archway of the Cathedral of Holy Exodus.

"And it is the seat of not more than a few heretical notions of doctrine, I'm afraid," he said in a low voice.

text1: Cathedral of Holy Exodus
text2:
Among towering columns were affixed recovered landing pods of the early Luddic refugee ships, now integrated into the vaults, painted with gilt and green, bedecked with candles and hanging glass ornaments which broke the beams of the sun - filtered through ranks of high windows - into thousands of rainbow shards endlessly refracting.

It was an ecstasy of light, a galaxy unto itself.

text1: Abyssal Light
text2: A cold light filled the viewscreen, stark against the utter darkness of the hyperspace abyss. It seemed to pulse, shifting dimmer and then brighter, following no discernable pattern. It did not flicker like a flame, nor like a failing comms signal, but like something flitting at the very edge of consciousness...

text1: Abyssal Light Encounter
text2:
Cold light began to grow in the primary display. A subtle pulse begged for attention.

A call of alarm, a flare of red, and a pause as the bridge officers took in the eruption of alerts that spilled across their display interfaces.

text1: Exotic Weapon Assembly
text2: Haz-suited technicians tended the containment machinery, stepping over coils of tube and wire. A mess of makeshift components were pulled from the common microforge libraries then torn apart and re-engineered to operate in this unusual, even extraordinary, application.

text1: Cargo Pods
text2:
Often filled with recoverable goods, even operational cryopods, a clutch of cargo pods in open space are a tempting target to scavengers. 

Trade convoys fleeing pirates sometimes dump cargo in the hope that their pursuers will power down their engines and content themselves with easy plunder.

text1: Coronal Tap, Interior
text2: These megastructures once powered the unfathomable industrial might of the Domain, putting the human civilization well toward Kardashev type II status. It is capable of extracting energy on an astronomical scale directly from the linked star.

text2: These megastructures once powered the unfathomable industrial might of the Domain, putting the human civilization well toward Kardashev type II status. It is capable of extracting energy on an astronomical scale directly from the linked star.

text1: Cryosleeper
text2: "Those who awake from cryosleep who were laid to rest in the time of the Domain are perhaps the most lost of all. Many, though not all, imagined they would populate the far end of a rich frontier; they accepted that they would leave behind all they once knew. Now they all find themselves cut loose from all reference, unmoored in history."

text1: Uncrewed Probe
text2: It was a common class of probe used for research, surveying, and surveillance, the sort of hardware used by civilian enterprise and research institutions. Often run without an active transponder so as not to invite opportunistic scavengers, an active ping will return the ID-packet required both under interfactional commerce law and by most credible insurance combines.

text1: A heavy ballistics and medium missile firepower-focused station module which effortlessly vaporizes smaller ships while giving cruisers and capitals good reason to maintain their distance. This well-armored and robustly engineered structure is designed to maximize compatibility with the low-fidelity construction typical of frontier nanoforge output.

A station module designed for close-volume power projection via two space fighter wings hangars and associated support facilities. Defended by respectable ballistics and medium missile firepower, this is a well-armored and robustly engineered structure designed to maximize compatibility with the low-fidelity construction typical of frontier nanoforge output.

Heavy battlestation armor is assembled in various forms from modular pieces to fit site-specific requirements. The primary structure, pierced only under conditions of heavy bombardment, consists of a custom semi-flexible high-tensile lattice filled with ultrahard nanoforged ceramic-metal composite blocks. Over this are placed alternating layers of foamed metal and 'wrap' consisting of many layers of sacrificial/reflective ablation skin.

This unit demonstrates the classic chevron arrangement.

Heavy battlestation armor is assembled in various forms from modular pieces to fit site-specific requirements. The primary structure, pierced only under conditions of heavy bombardment, consists of a custom semi-flexible high-tensile lattice filled with ultrahard nanoforged ceramic-metal composite blocks. Over this are placed alternating layers of foamed metal and 'wrap' consisting of many layers of sacrificial/reflective ablation skin.

This unit is an enlarged chevron arrangement.

Point-defense bastions, usually mounted on large booms extended from the primary volume of the station, enhance defenses against strike-craft, missiles, and frigates. This well-armored and robustly engineered structure is designed to maximize compatibility with the low-fidelity construction typical of frontier nanoforge output.

A heavy ballistics-focused citadel which mounts a broadside equivalent to a heavy battleship and its own self-contained flux grid. Usually placed on a spar extending from the primary volume of the station to provide overlapping fields of fire and, in the event of catastrophe, to minimize damage to the parent station. This well-armored and robustly engineered structure is designed to maximize compatibility with the low-fidelity construction typical of frontier nanoforge output.

An armored spar used to protect the flank of a station's primary batteries, this defense element is constructed of modular nanoforged components mounted on a tough yet relatively flexible structural lattice. Station armor is designed to find an ideal compromise between hardness, deflection, impact absorption, and heat dispersion across a wide range of combat scenarios, and as a rule permits for more generous mass allowance than could be reasonably mounted on a starship.

A very heavy station defense broadside module, this component alone demonstrates firepower comparable to any two standard line battleships. The combined mounting station segment - including this and counterweight defense and armor modules - is generally operated at a higher than usual rate of rotation to permit rapid on-target/recovery cycle.

A fighter support station module designed to enhance a station's local volume power-projection by mounting multiple fighter bays and missile launch points. This is a relatively soft target which nonetheless includes respectable point defense and armored hangars. A munitions depot, specialized on-site forges, and an isolated combat power system are situated on the inward-face.

A missile support bastion may be mounted on larger battlestations and star fortresses to enhance defense. This unit is designed to support other, direct firepower-focused modules by maintaining pressure and exploiting missile strike opportunities. A respectable point defense array discourages strike fighters while heavy armor allows for uninterrupted operation under heavy bombardment.

A dedicated advanced shield module designed to work as a specialized component in a whole-station defense matrix. Specialized shield modules with their own isolated combat reactors and flux grids allow firepower modules to vent without dropping defenses. This module is always paired and mounted on a rotating chassis so that station shields may alternate venting hard flux, theoretically creating an impenetrable defense.

An advanced armored spur which takes advantage of sophisticated nanoforge techniques. The plating of this module exploits unusual properties of quasicrystals and a 3D nanotubule mesh. This high-tech material science allows for armor-equivalent at lower mass and volume but the advantages are somewhat offset by the finicky manufacture and assembly process; specialist technicians are required to build this module and re-integrate replacement components after taking battle damage.

An advanced hangar module which may field four wings of fightercraft to dominate near-volume combat encounters. Due to a philosophy of specialization, this module does not have its own shield module; it is designed to work within the whole-station defense matrix. Nevertheless, enough directed energy firepower is mounted to discourage lighter warships. Integrates a fighter recall device, a specialized system originally created for the Astral-class carrier.

An advanced energy weapons battery, this specialized station defense module provides long-range firepower. Due to the whole-station defense matrix philosophy of design, no shield emitter is included; this means that the isolated combat reactors and flux grid can be dedicated entirely to offensive weapon systems.

A huge lunette designed to fill certain gaps discovered in the practical application of the whole-station defense matrix philosophy. The advanced plating requires sophisticated nanoforge production techniques which exploit unusual properties of quasicrystals and a 3D nanotubule mesh. This high-tech material science allows for equivalent armor protection at lower mass and volume than alternatives but the advantage is somewhat offset by the finicky manufacture and assembly process; specialist technicians are required to build this module and re-integrate replacement components after taking battle damage.

An advanced citadel possessing powerful shields, advanced armor, and bristling with energy weapons. This module was designed to fill certain gaps in the practical application of the specialized-module whole-station defense matrix philosophy. Because it extends beyond the coverage of dedicated shield modules, its own shield generator is an adaptation of the fortress shield system mounted on the Paragon-class battleship.

A small armored module which mounts a pair of small turrets usually used for asteroid defense and mining. It has no shield emitter, but this unit does possess a dedicated power-flux subsystem so that its mothership is not left defenseless in the case of emergency power failure.

An armored engine pod with its own power-flux subsystem and a small complement of mining and impactor defense turrets. Built to ensure that the mothership may operate even under the most hostile conditions across countless cycles.
A small gun-pod with its own backup reactor and flux grid. The turrets are generally dedicated to impactor defense.
A gun-pod which usually mounts a medium-class mining blaster so that the mothership can more efficiently harvest material necessary to produce additional exploration probes.

A gun-pod with a light complement of anti-impactor weapons and, usually, a medium-class mining blaster so that the mothership can more efficiently harvest material to produce additional exploration probes.

An exploration mothership bow sensor mast which mounts a respectable suite of anti-impactor turrets as well as a mining blaster to assist in material harvesting in support of the long-term mission of long-range survey. Firepower is light and mostly utilitarian, though it will discourage casual pirate-scavengers.
An armor module featuring high-tech nanoforged material technology. Seamless integration of peculiarly aesthetic macro-to-nanoscale fractal quasicrystal morphology suggests AI design.

A projecting advanced weapons platform of AI design. No internal life-support volumes; open-vacuum access tubes for maintenance drones are too small for human technicians. Features seamless integration of peculiarly aesthetic macro-to-nanoscale fractal quasicrystal morphology.

An advanced, AI-designed defense module utilizing a powerful shield and mounting dual Paladin PD turrets which will tear apart missiles and fighters alike with inhuman efficiency. Features seamless integration of peculiarly aesthetic macro-to-nanoscale fractal quasicrystal morphology.

An advanced, AI-designed support fighter hangar. Internal fighter repair and maintenance volumes are kept in near-vacuum and optimized to inhuman levels of efficiency due to lack of life support and safety requirements. PD coverage is modest; defensive turrets discourage strike fighters and opportunistic frigates. Armor features seamless integration of peculiarly aesthetic macro-to-nanoscale fractal quasicrystal morphology.

An advanced, AI-design firepower support weapon platform. Features dedicated combat generators and independent flux grid capable of powering primary large energy weapon battery and secondary medium-class energy turrets. Lack of life support requirements or habitable internal volumes enhances combat efficiency. Armor features seamless integration of peculiarly aesthetic macro-to-nanoscale fractal quasicrystal morphology.
A starship-sized structure of regular angular, geometric forms, albeit battle-damaged. It appears to consist largely of ferric alloys and has no active internal power source.

A rare, experimental prototype developed by the Tri-Tachyon Corporation, the Anubis-class hull has nearly full arc-coverage from three large energy turrets integrated into a controversial new flux grid. This 'new paradigm of flux management' is said to be directly interfaced with temporal manipulation technology of unknown provenance. Additional armament includes front-facing universal hardpoints and a fighter bay. The Anubis was unable to fulfill a robust multi-role tactical function due to poor flux performance so it is usually relegated to escort functions where, surprisingly, it excels.

The Anubis Project steering committee, keen to win support from as many departments as possible, drew upon new applications seeking customer validation from an exceptionally wide range of Tri-Tachyon's suite of breakthrough technologies. The project was then split into parallel operations at a handful of company black-sites to maximize security (and minimize oversight), leading to certain understandable late-stage compromises. It was hoped that the multi-functional product would cover more user-segments than one with more limited focus through a rough roll-out period. Then widespread field testing would surely enable rapid iteration which would lead, ultimately, to product maturity well ahead of conventional timelines.

This was not to be. Executive infighting led to a particularly dynamic org chart during the following financial cycle.
"If our particle accelerators could do that, it'd be like peeking into the mind of God."

My apologies. The physics team speculates ejecta is a stream of near-c charged matter-antimatter pseudoparticles annihilating in-situ in a... controlled, or perhaps semi-stable vortex. The baryonic asymmetry is unusual, to say the least."

When - not if - mesmerization manifests, the non-observing partner shall immediately execute remote display deactivation. This order stands until techops completes the automatic interrupt patch."
